overview: The Doxygen Filter extracts [Doxygen](http://www.stack.nl/~dimitri/doxygen/)-style comments from source code files for translation. It supports C++-style (`///`), Javadoc-style (`/**`), Qt-style (`/*!`), and Python-style (`'''` or `"""`) comment blocks. The full set of Doxygen special commands, HTML commands, and XML commands are recognized and interpreted as inline codes.

inlineCodes.help:
Controls how Doxygen special commands, HTML commands, and XML commands within comment blocks are handled as inline codes.

The filter automatically recognizes the full set of Doxygen [special commands](http://www.stack.nl/~dimitri/doxygen/commands.html), [HTML commands](http://www.stack.nl/~dimitri/doxygen/htmlcmds.html), and [XML commands](http://www.stack.nl/~dimitri/doxygen/xmlcmds.html). For example, `\class` and `\brief` are converted to inline codes like `<1/>` and `<2/>` in extracted text units.

parser.doxygenCommands.help:
Defines or overrides Doxygen command definitions used by the parser. Each entry is keyed by command name (without prefix, e.g. `code` for `\code{.py}`, case-sensitive) and specifies:

- **type**: `PLACEHOLDER`, `OPENING`, or `CLOSING`
- **inline**: `true` for inline, `false` for block-level (default: `false`)
- **pair**: For `OPENING`/`CLOSING` types, the paired command name (e.g. `code` pairs with `endcode`)
- **translatable**: Whether the command's content block is translatable (default: `true`)
- **parameters**: Ordered list of command parameters, each with `name`, `length` (`WORD`, `LINE`, `PHRASE`, or `PARAGRAPH`), `required` (default: `true`), and `translatable` (default: `true`)
notes: The `parameters` listing is optional. When present, parameters must be listed in the order they are written following the command.
Parameters with non-whitespace delimiters (e.g. `.py` in `\code{.py}`) are not currently supported.
Untranslatable parameters following translatable ones will be recorded as separate inline codes.
examples:
```yaml
doxygen_commands:
  code:
    type: OPENING
    inline: false
    pair: endcode
    translatable: false
    parameters:
      - name: language
        length: WORD
        required: false
        translatable: false
```
`PHRASE` length indicates a string bounded by double quotes, like `"image caption"`
`WORD`, `LINE`, `PHRASE`, `PARAGRAPH` map to the designations on the [Doxygen special commands page](http://www.stack.nl/~dimitri/doxygen/commands.html)

parser.htmlCommands.help:
Defines or overrides HTML command definitions used by the parser. Uses the same structure as `doxygenCommands` — each entry is keyed by command name and specifies `type`, `inline`, `pair`, `translatable`, and optional `parameters`.

You can also define custom regex-based commands:

```yaml
custom_commands:
  - pattern: "###ACCESS_CHECKS###.*?;"
    type: PLACEHOLDER
```

The `pattern` field accepts any valid regex matching non-zero-width text runs within comments. Matches are converted to codes using the same options as named commands.

parser.preserveWhitespace.help: Prevents the filter from collapsing whitespace in extracted text. By default, single linebreaks within a text run are collapsed into spaces, producing one potentially long line per translatable paragraph. Enabling this preserves original whitespace formatting.
notes: Without this option, each translatable paragraph is collapsed to a single (potentially very long) line with no effort to enforce a maximum line width on output.

limitations: Single linebreaks in a text run that are not part of a Doxygen command are collapsed. No maximum line width is enforced on output, so each translatable paragraph becomes a single long line.
Command parameters with non-whitespace delimiters (e.g. `.py` in `\code{.py}`) are not supported.
Non-translatable command parameters are not exposed for any special processing.
processingNotes: **Encoding detection**: If the file has a Unicode BOM, the corresponding encoding (UTF-8, UTF-16, etc.) is used. Otherwise, the default encoding specified when opening the document is used.
**Line numbers are preserved**: A one-to-one correspondence between source line number and translated line number is maintained.
Doxygen special commands, HTML commands, and XML commands are converted to inline codes (e.g. `<1/>`, `<2/>`) in extracted text units.

examples.title: Extracting Doxygen comments from C++ source
examples.description: Shows how various Doxygen comment styles are recognized and how commands are converted to inline codes during extraction.
examples.input:
```cpp
/*! \class Test class.h "inc/class.h"
 *  \brief This is a test class.
 *
 * Some details about the Test class
 */
```
examples.output:
```
Text Unit 1: <1/><2/> This is a test class.
Text Unit 2: Some details about the Test class
```

examples.title: Custom Doxygen command definition
examples.description: Defines a custom `mycommand` as an opening block-level command paired with `endmycommand`, with one required word parameter and one optional translatable phrase parameter.
examples.config:
```yaml
doxygen_commands:
  mycommand:
    type: OPENING
    inline: false
    pair: endmycommand
    translatable: true
    parameters:
      - name: id
        length: WORD
        required: true
        translatable: false
      - name: caption
        length: PHRASE
        required: false
        translatable: true
```

examples.title: Custom regex-based command
examples.description: Defines a custom command using a regex pattern to match specific text patterns within comments and treat them as placeholder codes.
